series_name: Baren Sump and The Last President
title: The Last President
subtitle: Book One
author: Alfred App
keywords: Baren Sump; political thriller; literary speculative fiction; prophecy infrastructure; dynasty; confession machine; media spectacle
short_description: When inventor Nikola Veyra dies in an unnatural storm, he leaves a scorched prediction card naming Baren Sump and THE LAST PRESIDENT.
long_description: When inventor Nikola Veyra dies in a storm that obeys no natural law, he leaves a prediction card scorched with impossible names—including Baren Sump and THE LAST PRESIDENT. What follows is not a chosen-one fable but a case file: algorithms that preach, families that buy tomorrow, confession machines that turn numbers into names, and a child who learns that being watched is not the same as being protected.

series_name: Baren Sump and The Last President
title: Children of Tomorrow
subtitle: Book Two
author: Alfred App
keywords: children are not evidence; Look-Away Ledger; legal thriller; accountability; child protection architecture; institutional fiction; trilogy
short_description: Protection ends. Accountability begins as one child's case expands into a system-wide reckoning.
long_description: Baren learns about a grounded plane from a cereal box. The Look-Away Ledger and the RAF-One archive turn one child's story into a system story. Courts without windows. Adults who were almost brave. The controlling sentence remains unbreakable: children are not evidence.

series_name: Baren Sump and The Last President
title: The Black Path
subtitle: Book Three
author: Alfred App
keywords: refusal; consent and availability; quiet ending; legal containment; literary thriller; ordinary life; black path
short_description: The black path is not escape—it is refusal, and ordinary life becomes the final claim.
long_description: The black path is not a tunnel—it is a refusal. Baren will not remain available as proof, witness, remedy, or adult repair tool. Book Three compresses toward a quiet ending: ordinary remains enough. No crowned climax. No child-saving-the-world turn.
